stimulus: Pitcher plants typically obtain nitrogen from the insects that fall into their fluid-filled traps, but certain species have also developed a partnership with tree shrews, small mammals that ______blank for the plants’ sugary nectar and leave nutrient-rich droppings behind.
stem: Which choice completes the text so that it conforms to the conventions of Standard English?
rationale:
Choice B is the best answer. The convention being tested is subject-verb agreement. The plural verb "visit" agrees in number with the plural relative-clause subject "that," whose antecedent is the plural noun "tree shrews."

Choice A is incorrect because the singular verb "has visited" doesn’t agree in number with the plural antecedent "tree shrews." Choice C is incorrect because the singular verb "visits" doesn’t agree in number with the plural antecedent "tree shrews." Choice D is incorrect because the singular verb "is visiting" doesn’t agree in number with the plural antecedent "tree shrews."
